description_full:
The Company
PayPal has been revolutionizing commerce globally for more than 25 years. Creating innovative experiences that make moving money, selling, and shopping simple, personalized, and secure, PayPal empowers consumers and businesses in approximately 200 markets to join and thrive in the global economy.

We operate a global, two-sided network at scale that connects hundreds of millions of merchants and consumers. We help merchants and consumers connect, transact, and complete payments, whether they are online or in person. PayPal is more than a connection to third-party payment networks. We provide proprietary payment solutions accepted by merchants that enable the completion of payments on our platform on behalf of our customers.

We offer our customers the flexibility to use their accounts to purchase and receive payments for goods and services, as well as the ability to transfer and withdraw funds. We enable consumers to exchange funds more safely with merchants using a variety of funding sources, which may include a bank account, a PayPal or Venmo account balance, PayPal and Venmo branded credit products, a credit card, a debit card, certain cryptocurrencies, or other stored value products such as gift cards, and eligible credit card rewards. Our PayPal, Venmo, and Xoom products also make it safer and simpler for friends and family to transfer funds to each other. We offer merchants an end-to-end payments solution that provides authorization and settlement capabilities, as well as instant access to funds and payouts. We also help merchants connect with their customers, process exchanges and returns, and manage risk. We enable consumers to engage in cross-border shopping and merchants to extend their global reach while reducing the complexity and friction involved in enabling cross-border trade.

Our beliefs are the foundation for how we conduct business every day. We live each day guided by our core values of Inclusion, Innovation, Collaboration, and Wellness. Together, our values ensure that we work together as one global team with our customers at the center of everything we do – and they push us to ensure we take care of ourselves, each other, and our communities.

Job Summary:

What you need to know about the role:

This is a Staff Experience Researcher role within PayPal’s EMEA Markets team, part of Global Customer Insights. You will lead market research across EMEA, with a focus on Germany, to uncover customer attitudes, competitive dynamics, and trends.

Meet our team:

The EMEA Markets team connects key markets, including the UK and Germany, with PayPal’s global Experience Research organization. We ensure local insights shape product strategy, localization, and customer experience. We partner across Consumer, Checkout, Financial Services, Merchant, and Global Product Solutions to bring the regional customer voice into global product direction.

Your way to impact:

You will lead quantitative market research programs, from opportunity scoping and launch diagnostics to concept tests and conjoint studies. Your insights will influence product roadmaps, go-to-market decisions, and leadership priorities tied to business outcomes.

Job Description:

Essential Responsibilities:


Develop clear plans and priorities to gather insights and make data-driven decisions to meet team performance
Perform more-complex analysis using highly specialized product knowledge and advanced business expertise
Lead functional projects and programs that enhance user experiences based on research findings.
Design methodologies including user testing, surveys and data analysis understanding of business needs and industry trends
Develop and implement research methodologies that optimize user testing and data collection processes.
Contribute to the continuous improvement of research practices and team efficiencies.


Minimum Qualifications:


5+ years relevant experience and a Bachelor’s degree OR Any equivalent combination of education and experience.


Additional Responsibilities & Preferred Qualifications:

Your day-to-day:


Design and execute strategic market research programs including launch diagnostics, conjoint analysis, concept testing, customer segmentation, and competitive intelligence studies for EMEA/Germany.
Develop and manage large-scale quantitative research—surveys, panels, and behavioral data integration—to generate statistically robust insights on customer attitudes, needs, and market trends.
Translate complex datasets into clear, compelling strategic narratives that drive decision-making at the product, regional, and executive level.
Partner with product, strategy, and commercial leadership to align research priorities with regional business goals and shape roadmap decisions.
Act as the EMEA subject matter expert in market research methodology—elevating standards, establishing best practices, and mentoring junior researchers across the team.


What do you need to bring:


7+ years of experience in market research, customer insights, or a closely related field, with a proven track record of delivering strategic, business-impacting insights.
Deep expertise in quantitative market research methodologies – concept tests, conjoint analysis, MaxDiff, discrete choice modeling, segmentation, and advanced survey design.
Strong analytical skills including statistical analysis, data interpretation, and the ability to integrate primary and secondary data sources (e.g., Qualtrics, SPSS, Python/R, Tableau/Power BI).
Experience working with EMEA/Germany markets and a strong understanding of regional consumer behavior, regulatory context, and cultural nuances.
Exceptional stakeholder management and executive communication skills, with the ability to influence cross-functional and senior decision-makers.


Preferred Qualifications:


Familiarity with financial services, payments, or fintech consumer landscapes in EMEA.
Experience managing research agencies and external vendors for large-scale quantitative programs.
English (professional level); German language proficiency (professional level) is a strong plus. Ideally additional language, such as French, Italian or Spanish.
Experience presenting to VP-level stakeholders.


What sets this role apart:


Focus on market-level strategic insights - not purely UX research - with direct influence on regional growth strategy.
High visibility with senior stakeholders across product, commercial, and regional leadership.
Opportunity to define and lead the quantitative research capability for EMEA within a globally influential insights organization.
Work at the intersection of customer understanding, competitive intelligence, and business strategy.


Subsidiary:

PayPal

Travel Percent:

0

PayPal does not charge candidates any fees for courses, applications, resume reviews, interviews, background checks, or onboarding. When making an application directly, we will never ask you to share passwords, one-time passcodes (OTP), or verification codes. Any such request is a red flag and likely part of a scam. All communication regarding your application will come from official PayPal email domains. If you suspect fraudulent activity, please report it immediately. To learn more about how to identify and avoid recruitment fraud please visit https://careers.pypl.com/contact-us.

For the majority of employees, PayPal's balanced hybrid work model offers 3 days in the office for effective in-person collaboration and 2 days at your choice of either the PayPal office or your home workspace, ensuring that you equally have the benefits and conveniences of both locations.

Our Benefits:

At PayPal, we’re committed to building an equitable and inclusive global economy. And we can’t do this without our most important asset-you. That’s why we offer comprehensive, choice-based programs, to support all aspects of personal wellbeing—physical, emotional, and financial—delivering meaningful value where it matters most. We strive to create a flexible, balanced work culture with a holistic approach to benefits, including generous paid time off, healthcare coverage for you and your family, and resources to create financial security and support your mental health.

Who We Are:

Belonging at PayPal:

Our employees are central to advancing our mission, and we strive to create an environment where everyone can do their best work with a sense of purpose and belonging. Belonging at PayPal means creating a workplace with a sense of acceptance and security where all employees feel included and valued. We are proud to have a diverse workforce reflective of the merchants, consumers, and communities that we serve, and we continue to take tangible actions to cultivate inclusivity and belonging at PayPal.

description_full:
Bei der Aktion Mensch erhebst und analysierst du nicht nur Daten – du bewegst mit deinen Erkenntnissen etwas wirklich Sinnvolles. Du erkennst, was hinter Zahlen steckt, machst deren Business-Relevanz sichtbar und legst damit die Grundlage für strategische Entscheidungen mit echter Wirkung.




Tu, was du liebst

Im dreiköpfigen Team, das eine Stabsstelle beim Vorstand ist, bist du als Senior-Expert*in dafür verantwortlich, zu analysieren, welche Projekte Inklusion tatsächlich verbessern, wie erfolgreich Lotteriekampagnen sind und wo Förderung die größte Wirkung entfaltet – und leitest daraus gezielt Maßnahmen für maximale gesellschaftliche Wirkung ab.




Du erkennst die strategische Relevanz von Marktdaten und Insights, übersetzt diese in belastbare, entscheidungsrelevante Empfehlungen und lieferst damit die Grundlage für eine wirkungsvolle Markt- und Markenstrategie.
Du verantwortest die End-to-End-Konzeption, Planung und Steuerung anspruchsvoller Marktforschungsprojekte entlang zentraler Business-Fragestellungen – von Marke, Kommunikation und Kampagnen bis hin zu Förderung, Pricing und Produkt.
Als zentrale Ansprechperson und Sparringspartner*in berätst du interne Stakeholder auf allen Ebenen (u. a. Lotterie, Förderung, Kampagne, Kommunikation) zu marktforschungsrelevanten Fragestellungen und entwickelst passgenaue, methodisch fundierte Studiendesigns–von pragmatischen Set-ups bis hin zu komplexen, multimethodalen Ansätzen.
Du analysierst Primär- und Sekundärdaten mit hoher methodischer Sicherheit, identifizierst zentrale Treiber und Muster und übersetzt diese in klare, entscheidungsrelevante Handlungsempfehlungen.
Du bereitest Insights zielgruppenspezifisch auf und sorgst für maximale Wirkung – in Management-Präsentationen, Workshops sowie in strukturierten Reporting-Formaten.
Du priorisierst Projekte entlang strategischer Relevanz, triffst fundierte methodische Entscheidungen und stellst eine hohe Qualität, Effizienz und Termintreue in der Umsetzung sicher.
Die unternehmensweiten Marktforschungsstandards entwickelst du weiter, etablierst eine zukunftsfähige Forschungsarchitektur und treibst deren Umsetzung voran.
Du dentifizierst relevante Markt- und Methoden-Trends, evaluierstinnovative Ansätze und initiierst Pilotprojekte– insbesondere im Hinblick auf den Einsatzneuer Technologien wie KI in der Marktforschung.
Du steuerst Marktforschungsinstitute und Dienstleister, verantwortest Budgets und stellst Transparenz sowie Controlling sicher.
Du gibstjuniorigenKolleg*innen fachliche Orientierung und arbeitest eng mit interdisziplinären Teams zusammen.

Tech Stack: Zeotap Customer Data Platform, Holistics BI, Google Analytics 4, Tealium iQ, EasyFeedback, GetFeedback, SPSS




Zeig, was du kannst

Deine Leidenschaft ist die Marktforschung und du bringst mehrjährige (ca.4–6+Jahre) fundierte Erfahrung in der betrieblichen Marktforschung, im Insights-Umfeld oder auf Agenturseite mit und verstehst es, Forschung konsequent an Business- und Wirkungszielen auszurichten.
Du verfügst über ein sehr breites und tiefes Methodenverständnis – sowohl im quantitativen als auch im qualitativen Bereich – und wählst je nach Fragestellung souverän den passenden methodischen Ansatz.
Du hast nachweislich Erfahrung in der Steuerung komplexer Marktforschungsprojekte – inklusive Budgetverantwortung, Dienstleistersteuerung und Qualitätssicherung.
Du überzeugst durch ausgeprägte analytische Stärke und die Fähigkeit, Daten in fundierte, strategisch relevante Handlungsempfehlungen zu übersetzen.
Du agierst sicher auf Augenhöhe mit unterschiedlichen Stakeholdern und bringst ausgeprägte Beratungs-, Moderations- und Kommunikationskompetenz mit.
Du verfolgst aktiv neue Entwicklungen in Marktforschung, Methoden und Technologien (z. B. datengetriebene Ansätze, Automatisierung, KI) und hast Freude daran, diese konzeptionell weiterzudenken und einzusetzen.
Du arbeitest strukturiert, eigenverantwortlich und priorisierst souverän in einem dynamischen, anspruchsvollen Umfeld.
Du hast ein abgeschlossenes Hochschulstudium, z. B. in Marktforschung, Wirtschaft‑oder Sozialwissenschaften, Psychologie, Marketing oder einem vergleichbaren Studiengang.




Unser Erfolgsrezept: Das WIR gewinnt

Ein Job, in dem man mit viel Spaß und freier Zeiteinteilung Gutes tun kann. Gleitzeit oder mobiles Arbeiten unterstützen dabei, Beruf und Privatleben zu vereinbaren (60 %mobiles Arbeitund 40 % Büropräsenz).
Persönliche und fachliche Weiterentwicklung durch individuelle Fortbildungen, Coaching, Leadership-Programme, Masterplan und Entwicklungspläne.
Positives, offenes und freundliches Arbeitsumfeld. Wertschätzende❤️-Atmosphäre, klare Führung und die Möglichkeit, aus Fehlern zu lernen, sind selbstverständlich sowie ein nachhaltiges Arbeitsumfeld.
Eine moderne IT-Ausstattung, barrierefreie Arbeitsplätze, technische Hilfen, ein Leitsystem gehören ganz selbstverständlich dazu und ermöglichen ein entspanntes, professionelles Arbeiten. Assistenzen und Assistenzhunde sind ebenso selbstverständlich willkommen und Teil unseres täglichen Miteinanders.
Attraktives Gehalt,6,7 % Erfolgsbeteiligung (vom Jahresbruttogehalt), kostenfreies Deutschlandticket, umfangreiche Sozialleistungen (zusätzliches Kindergeld, betriebliche Altervorsorge etc.)

Inklusion ist unser Herzensanliegen. Als größte private Förderorganisation begeistern wir die Menschen für eine Gesellschaft, in der Unterschiede normal sind. description_full:
Wir suchen für unser Team in München oder Berlin eine Junior Research & Insights Manager (w/m/d) befristet auf 18 Monate.

Wir sind eine globale, socially-led Creative Agency mit über 1.300 Mitarbeitenden in 17 Büros weltweit. In einer Welt, in der Social Media Kultur prägt, helfen wir Marken dabei, Ideen zu entwickeln, über die Menschen sprechen.

Wir verstehen Online-Communities, Kulturen und Subkulturen über die gesamte Social-Landschaft hinweg. Zu unseren Kund:innen in Deutschland zählen unter anderem AUDI AG, Mercedes-Benz Trucks und Müller. We Are Social ist Teil der Plus Company.

Als Junior Research & Insights Manager (w/m/d) unterstützt du unser Intelligence Team bei unterschiedlichen Kund:innenprojekten und arbeitest eng mit erfahrenen Kolleg:innen aus Research, Strategie und Data zusammen. Dabei lernst du Schritt für Schritt, wie datenbasierte Insights entstehen und wie sie kreative und strategische Entscheidungen unterstützen.

Requirements

Deine Aufgaben:


Unterstützung bei der Recherche und Analyse von Social-Media-, Markt- und Zielgruppendaten
Mitarbeit bei der Erstellung von Reports, Präsentationen und Benchmark-Analysen
Unterstützung bei der Pflege und Aktualisierung von Dashboards
Aufbereitung und Visualisierung von Daten und Erkenntnissen für interne und externe Präsentationen
Beobachtung von digitalen Trends, Plattformen und kulturellen Entwicklungen
Unterstützung bei der Analyse von Creator- und Influencer-Aktivitäten
Zusammenarbeit mit dem Strategy-, Research- und Data-Team bei unterschiedlichen Kund:innenprojekten
Schrittweises Lernen und Verstehen von KPI-Analysen, Insight-Entwicklung und datenbasierter Strategiearbeit


Wonach suchen wir?


Erste praktische Erfahrung im Agentur-, Marketing-, Research- oder Social-Media-Umfeld
Großes Interesse an Social Media, digitalen Trends und Online-Kultur
Interesse an Daten, Kommunikation, Insights und Konsumentenverhalten
Motivation, neue Tools und Plattformen schnell zu lernen
Strukturierte, zuverlässige und eigenständige Arbeitsweise
Kommunikationsstärke und Spaß an Teamarbeit
Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift


Benefits

Warum du Teil unseres Teams werden solltest?

Lebendiger Teamgeist: Wir tragen das Adjektiv social sehr bewusst im Namen und leben einen starken Teamgeist und eine offene Unternehmenskultur mit flachen Hierarchien, in denen deine Stimme zählt!

Spannende Projekte mit internationaler Reichweite: Wir arbeiten an digitalen Projekten, die Maßstäbe setzen! Unsere Kund:innen kommen aus dem B2C- und B2B-Umfeld und sind sowohl lokale als auch internationale Player.

Global verbunden und lokal verwurzelt: Unsere Teams an den Standorten München und Kreuzberg profitieren von einem nachhaltigen Wissensaustausch mit den WAS Standorten weltweit und sind im Glockenbachviertel und Kreuzberg zu Hause.

Flexibilität - Yes please!: Hybrides Arbeiten vereint für uns die Vorteile des Home Offices mit denen des Arbeitens im Büro. Genau deswegen haben wir uns für einen 2/3 Split entschieden, der bedeutet, dass wir bei 40h mindestens 2 Tage vor Ort mit unseren Kolleg:innen und 3 Tage im Home Office arbeiten. But that's not all! Darüber hinaus sind wir große Fans von Remote Arbeit. Deswegen hast du bei uns die Möglichkeit 20 Tage innerhalb Deutschlands und weitere 20 Tage innerhalb Europas remote zu arbeiten. Wenn du also kein großer Fan des Deutschen Winters bist, join us und du kannst diese Zeit in Portugal oder Griechenland genießen.

Mehr Benefits für dich: Weil wir wissen, wie wichtig Well-being ist und wir Benefits einfach lieben, bieten wir dir mehr als nur den Standard. Genieße 2 zusätzliche Health Days, Menstrual Leave, einen freien Tag an deinem Geburtstag, eine Kooperation mit Wellpass, das Deutschlandticket, eine Mitgliedschaft bei OpenUp und vieles mehr.

description_full:
Vollgas nicht nur auf der Straße – sondern auch mit uns im Job!

Die Stellantis Germany GmbH ist Teil des weltweiten Automobilkonzerns Stellantis N.V.

In Deutschland ist die Stellantis Germany GmbH aus den beiden Unternehmensteilen

FCA Germany GmbH und Groupe PSA Deutschland GmbH hervorgegangen. Unter dem Dach von Stellantis werden die Marken Abarth, Alfa Romeo, Citroën, DS Automobiles, Fiat, Fiat Professional (leichte Nutzfahrzeuge), Jeep®, Leapmotor, Opel und Peugeot vertrieben.




Der/die Market Funnel Performance Manager:in verantwortet die Full-Funnel-Performance im jeweiligen Markt und stellt eine enge Verzahnung zwischen regionalen Standards und marktspezifischen Anforderungen sicher. Die Rolle aktiviert regionale Methodiken, Dashboards und KPIs und adaptiert die Umsetzung entlang des lokalen Konsumentenverhaltens, der Channel-Realität sowie der Business-Dynamik.

Sie/Er stellt sicher, dass Performance-Entscheidungen im Markt datengetrieben erfolgen – mit regionalem Reporting als primärem Framework, angereichert um kontextuelle lokale Insights.

Zudem speist die Rolle Markt-Learnings zurück, um regionale Playbooks, Benchmarks und zukünftige Guidelines weiterzuentwickeln.




Kernaufgaben

1. Full-Funnel-Ownership für den Markt

Steuert die Markt-Performance über alle Funnel-Stufen hinweg:
Awareness → Consideration → Intent → Conversion → Loyalty.
Übersetzt regionale Frameworks in marktrelevante Strategien.
Priorisiert Performance-Treiber basierend auf Markt-Insights, Wettbewerbsumfeld und Funnel-Diagnostik.




2. Alignment mit regionalen Standards & Tools

Implementiert regionale KPI-Definitionen, Methodiken, Dashboards und Governance-Prozesse.
Stellt sicher, dass der Markt regionale Test-Frameworks, Messstandards und Reporting-Zyklen konsequent anwendet.
Arbeitet mit regionalen Teams an der Weiterentwicklung von Benchmarks, Target-Setting und Methodik-Updates.




3. Markt-Insights als Input für regionale Teams

Liefert strukturierte Markt-Insights, Performance-Treiber, Test-Learnings und Business-Kontext.
Hebt marktspezifische Besonderheiten hervor, die Funnel-KPIs und Optimierungsansätze beeinflussen.
Gestaltet zukünftige regionale Guidelines durch Feedback aus der lokalen Umsetzung mit.




4. Funnel-Optimierung & cross-funktionale Steuerung

Leitet Funnel-Diagnostik und Maßnahmenpläne über Media, LFAs, CRM, Markt- und Brand-Teams hinweg.
Steuert Agenturen zur Optimierung von Creatives, Audiences, Customer Journeys und Conversion-Flows.
Treibt A/B-Testing im Einklang mit regionalen Protokollen – zugeschnitten auf lokale Kanäle.




5. Business-Governance & Reporting

Stellt sicher, dass regionale Dashboards als Single Source of Truth für Market Business Reviews genutzt werden.
Unterstützt Management-Entscheidungen durch KPI-Interpretation und lokalen Performance-Kontext.
Bereitet marktbezogene Insights für wöchentliche, monatliche und quartalsweise Performance-Reviews auf.




6. Budgetsteuerung & Forecasting

Wendet regionale Media-Forecasting-Modelle an – angereichert um lokale Marktindikatoren.
Überwacht Spend-Effizienz und empfiehlt Budget-Reallokationen entlang der Funnel-Stufen.
Treibt die Steigerung der Conversion-Effizienz.




Zentrale Funnel-KPIs

(Im Einklang mit regionalen Definitionen – angewendet im jeweiligen Markt-Kontext)

Awareness: Reach, CPR, Traffic-Qualität
Consideration: CTR, Engagement, Lead-Volumen, CPL
Intent & Conversion: Lead-to-Sale-Conversion, CPA
Retention: Loyalty-Rate, CRM-Lifecycle-KPIs
Effizienz: Budget-Allokations-Effizienz
Forecasting: Genauigkeit der Demand-/Lead-/Sales-Forecasts




Skills & Kompetenzen

Ausgeprägte analytische Fähigkeiten und strategisches Denken
Expertise in Funnel-Performance, Digital Marketing und Media-Optimierung
Fähigkeit, Daten in umsetzbare Business-Insights zu übersetzen
Stakeholder-Management über Markt- und regionale Teams hinweg
Sicherer Umgang mit Analytics-Tools (GA4, Power BI/Tableau, CRM- & Media-Plattformen)
Starke Kommunikationsfähigkeit sowie Decision Enablement-Skills




Erfahrung

6–10+ Jahre in Performance Marketing, Digital Analytics oder Growth-Rollen
Nachweisliche Erfahrung im Management von Full-Funnel-KPIs
Erfahrung in der Arbeit innerhalb marktübergreifender bzw. regionaler Frameworks
Führungserfahrung mit Agenturen und cross-funktionalen Teams




Interesse geweckt? Wir freuen uns auf Ihre Bewerbung!

Sie erwartet eine attraktive und herausfordernde Tätigkeit eines dynamischen und internationalen Automobilkonzerns. Wenn Sie sich durch unsere Stellenausschreibung angesprochen fühlen, dann freuen wir uns auf Ihre aussagekräftige Bewerbung (Motivationsschreiben, Lebenslauf, Zeugnisse) unter Angabe Ihres frühestmöglichen Eintrittstermins und Gehaltswunsches.




Bei Stellantis beurteilen wir Kandidaten auf der Grundlage ihrer Qualifikationen, Verdienste und unseren unternehmerischen Anforderungen. Wir freuen uns über Bewerbungen von Menschen aller Geschlechtsidentitäten, Altersgruppen, ethnischen Zugehörigkeiten, Nationalitäten, Religionen, sexuellen Orientierungen und Behinderungen. Vielfältige Teams machen es möglich, die sich verändernden Bedürfnisse unserer Kunden besser zu erfüllen und für unsere Zukunft zu sorgen.

description_full:
The Position

Join Boehringer Ingelheim’s Animal Health organization as a key expert driving the transformation of regional forecasting and demand planning processes. In this role, you will contribute to excellence in forecast performance, process efficiency, and system adoption while collaborating closely with cross‑functional partners to support strategic business needs.

Tasks & Responsibilities


In this role, you will lead regional demand forecasting by orchestrating, validating, and continuously improving statistically robust forecasts, integrating market intelligence, commercial insights, and local risks and opportunities in line with global standards
Within your area of responsibility, you will own the monthly demand forecasting cadence, ensuring strong governance, high data quality, and clear documentation of assumptions and rationale
By analyzing forecast performance, you will drive continuous improvement in forecast accuracy and bias through targeted corrective actions
As part of the regional organization, you will act as the forecasting business partner for commercial, finance, marketing, and supply chain stakeholders, ensuring transparent communication on assumptions, risks, scenarios, and opportunities
When supporting Regional and Global Demand Reviews, you will challenge inputs, highlight deviations, and escalate key risks where required
With a strong focus on data integrity, you will govern data quality, reporting, and insights, ensuring reliable master and transactional data within forecasting systems
By delivering executive‑level insights, you will ensure consistent KPI logic within the “Measure What Matters” framework and provide reporting via SAC and Power BI



Requirements


A Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Supply Chain Management, Business Analytics, Data Science, Statistics, or a related field
Several years of professional experience in forecasting, demand planning, or supply chain management, ideally within bio‑pharmaceutical, animal health, or FMCG environments
Advanced expertise in forecasting technologies such as SAP‑IBP, Kinaxis, or JDA, combined with strong knowledge of statistical modeling and predictive analytics
The ability to clearly communicate complex, data‑driven strategies to senior leaders and top management in global, cross‑cultural settings
Proven experience in leading and governing end‑to‑end S&OP or IBP processes at regional or global scale, translating analytical insights into business recommendations with impact on regional P&L
Fluency in English is required; German language skills and professional certifications such as APICS or IBF are considered an advantage



Applications from persons with severe disabilities are warmly welcomed. In cases of equal qualifications, such applicants will be given preferential consideration in the selection process.

Ready to contact us?

If you have any questions about the job posting or process - please contact our HR Direct Team, Tel: +49 (0) 6132 77-3330 or via mail: hr.de@boehringer-ingelheim.com

Recruitment process:

Step 1: Online application - The job posting is presumably online until "June 16th, 2026". We reserve the right to take the posting offline beforehand. Applications up to "June 3rd, 2026" are guaranteed to be considered.

Step 2: Virtual meeting in the period from beginning till mid of June

Step 3: On-site interviews mid/end of June

Please submit your application documents in English.

Our Company

Why Boehringer Ingelheim?

With us, you can develop your own path in a company with a culture that knows our differences are our strengths - and break new ground in the drive to make millions of lives better.

Here, your development is our priority. Supporting you to build a career as part of a workplace that is independent, authentic and bold, while tackling challenging work in a respectful and friendly environment where everyone is valued and welcomed.

Alongside, you have access to programs and groups that ensure your health and wellbeing are looked after - as we make major investments to drive global accessibility to healthcare. By being part of a team that is constantly innovating, you'll be helping to transform lives for generations.

description_full:
ZEAL Network SE is a dynamic, international group of companies that has been creating exciting and innovative lottery experiences for over 20 years. As Germany’s largest provider of online lotteries through our brands LOTTO24 and Tipp24, and a trusted partner of Spain’s ONCE, the second-largest national lottery, we’re shaping the future of the lottery industry. We are passionate about driving innovation, as demonstrated by our newest brands, Freiheit+ and Traumhausverlosung, which bring fresh and exciting dreams to life. Additionally, through ZEAL Ventures, we invest in inspiring e-commerce start-ups like Omaze and DAYMADE, fueling fresh ideas and opportunities.

Our team of 350+ professionals from diverse backgrounds is united by a visionary mindset, a passion for collaboration, and a commitment to inspire dreams. At ZEAL, we empower our people to think ambitiously and work together toward a shared purpose: helping people imagine and achieve their aspirations.

The Role

As the Data Product Manager - Marketing Analytics (f/m/d), you will own the marketing analytics domain end to end. You are the senior voice on what gets built, why it matters, and in what order. You partner directly with marketing leadership to translate ambition into a clear, prioritised roadmap, and you lead the team that delivers it.

This is a domain ownership role, not a coordination role. You set direction, manage trade-offs, and represent the marketing analytics agenda in executive conversations. You treat the domain as a portfolio of data products with defined owners, users, success metrics, and lifecycles. You are accountable for outcomes, not activity.

You will also be the senior measurement authority for marketing performance at ZEAL, with hands-on credibility across MMM, attribution, and incrementality. You hold vendors accountable, build internal capability, and ensure decisions on marketing investment are grounded in defensible analytics.

Your Tasks


The marketing analytics roadmap as domain owner, including prioritisation, sequencing, trade-offs, and alignment with marketing leadership and broader ZEAL strategy.
Stakeholder communication across the marketing analytics agenda, translating business ambition into structured plans, managing expectations proactively, and saying no constructively when capacity or strategy requires it.
Day-to-day leadership of the marketing analytics team, including analysts, data scientists, analytics engineers, and data engineers working within the domain. You grow their capability and protect their focus.
Marketing measurement maturity across MMM, attribution, and incrementality. You own methodology decisions, design experiments and incrementality tests, evaluate vendors, and build internal capability rather than outsourcing it.
Partnership with marketing leadership on planning, investment decisions, performance reviews, and campaign analytics, ensuring data is in the room when decisions are made.
The data product mindset for your domain. You define products with clear owners, users, success metrics, and lifecycles, rather than treating analytics as a queue of requests.
Portfolio discipline across the domain, including scope, risk, dependencies, and transparency. You make it easy for executives to see what is happening and why.
Partnership with the Tech Lead spine on standards, architecture, and engineering practice. You own WHAT and WHY, they own HOW. You respect that line.


What You Offer Us


8+ years of experience in analytics, with at least 3 years leading a team, ideally in e-commerce, subscription, or comparable consumer business contexts.
Hands-on depth in marketing measurement, including MMM, attribution, and incrementality. You can challenge methodology, design experiments, and hold vendors accountable, not just consume their outputs.
Fluency with marketing tech and AdTech stacks, and a working understanding of how marketing systems produce the data you analyse.
Track record building, growing, and retaining strong analytics teams across analysts, scientists, and engineers.
Genuine product mindset. You think in terms of data products with defined users and success metrics, not reports and dashboards.
Strong programme discipline. You manage portfolios, surface risks early, and create clarity across multiple concurrent initiatives.
Executive presence and stakeholder communication skills that hold up at VP and C-level. You can align senior leaders, push back without damaging trust, and create structure where there is pressure.
University degree in a relevant field, or equivalent demonstrable experience.


What We Offer You


A modern, dynamic culture grounded in the success of a leading-edge e-commerce company.
A talented team of ambitious, like-minded colleagues where your knowledge and ideas truly make an impact.
Ongoing personal growth, supported by an annual development budget of €2,500 to invest in your professional development.
Trust and autonomy, no micromanagement, just confidence in your ability to deliver and excel.
Flexible work options with a hybrid setup focused on results.
Unlimited vacation days in addition to your yearly 30-day vacation allowance.
Opportunity for workations abroad using our WorkFlex tool.
Monthly mobility allowance of €30 to use on the mobility option of your choice (e.g., the Deutschlandticket), plus access to a company bike leasing program.
Company pension scheme, provided in partnership with Degura, to support your future financial security.
Subsidized EGYM WELLPASS membership, with a contribution of only €25 per month, giving you access to a wide range of gyms and sports options across Germany.
Monthly company events, team activities, and informal gatherings, including our popular company-wide lunches provided four days a week.

description_full:
About Agoda

At Agoda, we bridge the world through travel. Our story began in 2005, when two lifelong friends and entrepreneurs, driven by their passion for travel, launched Agoda to make it easier for everyone to explore the world.

Today, we are part of Booking Holdings [NASDAQ: BKNG], with a diverse team of over 7,000 people from 90 countries, working together in offices around the globe. Every day, we connect people to destinations and experiences, with our great deals across our millions of hotels and holiday properties, flights, and experiences worldwide.

No two days are the same at Agoda. Data and technology are at the heart of our culture, fueling our curiosity and innovation. If you’re ready to begin your best journey and help build travel for the world, join us.

Get to know our team:

The Marketing team at Agoda is highly data-driven and focused on developing at-scale, measurable marketing programs designed to improve the lifetime value of Agoda customers. The team combines a blend of the best analysts, marketing strategists and data scientists, collectively building a team that thrives on data, creative ideas and technology. . The Marketing leadership fosters a great learning environment, providing the opportunity to learn and grow by working closely with experts from a diverse variety of backgrounds from across the globe.

Due to continued expansion, there are multiple analyst roles within the marketing team across different marketing channels. While the scope will vary depending on these channels, here are some examples:


Experimentation and optimizing campaign performance: Experiment with ads/campaign structures and bidding& pricing strategies on partners such as Google, Bing, Tripadvisor, Trivago and other search engines. Adapt to new product features and roll out changes from successful tests.
Modeling: Analyze vast amounts of data generated by experiments, develop predictive models using data science techniques (eg. understanding impact on bookings from large scale TV campaigns or demand elasticity from pricing optimization) and liaise with product teams on implementation roadmap
Reporting, analysis and insights: Building dashboards to track performance, derive insights, understand growth levers and communicate recommendations via presentations to stakeholders


What you’ll Need to Succeed:


Bachelor’s degree or higher from top university in a quantitative subject (computer science, mathematics, engineering business or science)
Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English
2+ years experience in data crunching from top-tier consulting, investment banking, private equity or strategy/business for a fast-growing globaltech company
Experience in one or more data analysis packages or databases, eg., SAS, R, SPSS, Python, VBA, SQL and visualization tools, eg. Tableau, Power BI
Ability to move fast and be efficient, making decisions on objective evidence rather than subjective gut feel
Innate desire to take ownership, make an impact and influence outcomes
Excellent organizational skills, attention to detail and ability to work independently


It’s great if you have:


Experience in digital marketing or ecommerce
Experience with A/B testing and other testing metrics
Strong presentation and negotiation skills
Skills that foster collaboration and team spirit

description_full:
Responsibilities
The Measurement team is responsible for interpreting the performance of advertising on our platform. We work with advertisers to optimise their activity on the platform and build meaningful connections that help drive behaviour change.

About the Role
This Measurement Partner role partners with leading clients in the designated region, supporting a mix of branding-focused as well as app and web performance-driven advertisers, to help measure the effectiveness of their investment in our platform with performance-focussed advertisers. In addition, the Measurement Partner partners with key cross-functional teams such as sales, product marketing, measurement product, and business marketing to ensure that clients and agencies have best-in-class support.

Highly-driven and empathetic, the Measurement Partner will build on our Measurement discipline where execution, curiosity, and data-driven thinking in a fast-growth environment to make others better is critical for success.

Responsibilities:
- Partner primarily with clients and agencies to provide good measurement that can lead to better understanding of their performance on the platform
- Focus on strategic consulting together with revenue and product teams to help drive and implement learning agendas for clients across branding, app and web performance use cases
- Analyse data to develop advanced analytics projects tailor made for clients to prove that TikTok works
- Manage client and agency feedback to ensure their voice is reflected with our product and partnerships teams

Qualifications
Minimum Qualifications:
- Extensive, proven experience with digital advertising, large marketing-related organizations, or measurement suppliers
- Relevant bachelor's degree; or equivalent in a quantitative field.
- A track record of inferential problem solving through experimental design and analytical methods
- Exceptional time-management and goal-oriented task management skills are required
- Familiarity with experimentation, incrementality testing as well as attribution and Media Mix Modelling, including triangulation across these methodologies
- Familiarity with digital analytics tools such as Google Analytics, Adobe Analytics
- Exposure to e-commerce-heavy advertisers that are heavily engaging in app and/or web performance measurement is essential

Preferred Qualifications:
- Graduate degree within a science, mathematics, operations, business, digital marketing, or research field preferred
- Experience with advertising agencies or complex marketing data-driven organizations
- Familiarity with privacy-first innovation for measurement systems
- Experience with executing custom analytical work

TikTok is the leading destination for short-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.​

Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.​
​
Diversity & Inclusion​
TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. Unsere Kultur und das Zusammenarbeiten sind geprägt von gegenseitiger Wertschätzung, Anerkennung und Unterstützung. Das verbindet uns hierarchieübergreifend – auch im Home-Office. Unser umfangreiches Trainingsangebot und ein transparentes Laufbahnstufenmodell für alle sorgen dafür, dass deine Entwicklung bei uns nicht stillsteht. description_full:
Data Scientist – Analytics, Statistics & BI - €130,000




A growing AI-first HealthTech company in the Frankfurt Rhine-Main region is hiring a Data Scientist - Analytics, Statistics & BI.




This is not a pure ML engineering role. It’s a senior analytics position focused on rigorous statistical thinking, production data systems, and real-world healthcare impact.




You’ll take ownership of the analytics layer behind a live AI platform already used in therapeutic environments. Working with complex behavioural and clinical datasets, you’ll apply strong statistical methodologies to identify meaningful patterns, evaluate study outcomes, and translate data into insights that directly influence product and clinical decision-making.




This is a senior individual contributor role with real influence - partnering closely with AI, engineering and product teams while driving BI, data quality standards, and the evolution of the data warehouse environment.




Why join?

Meaningful AI impact in pediatric HealthTech
Live production system with real-world usage
High ownership and visibility in a 30-person company
Hybrid setup in the Frankfurt Rhine-Main region
Bonus, stock options, 30 days holiday + strong benefits package




What you’ll bring:

5+ years’ experience as a Data Scientist with strong analytics and statistical focus
Excellent foundation in applied statistics (hypothesis testing, regression modelling, study evaluation)
Strong Python and solid SQL
Experience working with modern data warehouses (BigQuery ideal)
Experience owning or evolving BI environments (Superset, Looker, Power BI etc.)
AI-ready mindset – actively leveraging modern AI tools to improve workflows




If you’re a statistically rigorous, product-minded Data Scientist who wants ownership and genuine impact - let’s talk.




Keywords: Senior Data Scientist, Analytics, Statistical Modelling, Python, SQL, BigQuery, GCP, Business Intelligence, HealthTech, AI Systems

description_full:
Your job at DKV Mobility? As part of a leading European B2B platform for on-the-road payment solutions, you will work in an exciting environment. Our tasks are defined by our Purpose: To drive the transition towards an efficient and sustainable future of mobility. We offer you numerous opportunities to help shape the mobility of tomorrow in a dynamic and agile environment. Our approximately 2,700 employees throughout Europe are united by a common passion: to give their best every day for our customers in more than 50 countries. True to our claim: You drive, we care. Become part of our diverse, dedicated and unique team!

What you can expect

Would you like to actively contribute to a company’s growth and help turn ambitious plans into reality? Then join our team! You can expect exciting challenges, an inspiring environment, and a work culture that combines flexibility and collaboration – supported by our 60/40 working model and modern offices in Ratingen as a hub for ideas. As part of our team, you will take on the following responsibilities:


You define, design, and develop cloud-based data products (Core Data Products or Analytical Data Products).
You are responsible for the systematic integration of data assets and data products and define documentation standards in collaboration with the entire Data Intelligence & Analytics team.
You take product ownership for the data products of the Data Intelligence & Analytics team.
You actively promote cross-functional collaboration with the Business Data Hubs to develop data use cases together with relevant stakeholders.
You ensure data and product quality in close cooperation with the Data Governance team and the Business Data Hubs.
You manage, coordinate, and prioritize the backlog of your data products from a functional perspective.


What makes you stand out


You have experience as a Data Product Owner, Product Owner, or in a comparable role, with a background in Data Engineering and/or Data Science.
You have a strong understanding of data products, analytics architectures, and data assets.
You have experience working with interdisciplinary teams across business, data, and IT.
You possess very good knowledge of agile methodologies and common prioritization frameworks.
You work in an analytical and structured manner and communicate clearly and convincingly.
You stand out through a high level of ownership, a strong sense of responsibility, and a strong commitment to quality.


We are looking forward to your application and to applicants who enrich our diverse culture! Regardless of age, gender, origin and sexual identity and orientation. Severely disabled applicants will be given preference if equally qualified. This position can be full-time or part-time (min. 30h/week). DKV Mobility is looking for the best talents and knows that these are not always available on a full-time basis. Many of our employees work part-time or in flexible working models. Please talk to us about the flexibility you need during the application process.

Why DKV Mobility? Here are five good reasons...

At DKV Mobility, we are team players - because we are only successful together! #teamofteams

You should feel welcome with us from the very beginning!

We've Been Growing For About 90 Years

How we do it? We keep adapting our products and business model to meet changing customer needs. That's how we stay mobile and master every challenge the future holds.

There are also many other good reasons to work at DKV Mobility. For example, we offer you comprehensive company health management, parent-child offices (from 2023) and support you in leasing your eBike through our partner Jobrad!

We attach great importance to work-life balance.

We stand for the compatibility of family and career! That's why we offer flexible working hours and family-friendly working time models. In addition, we rely on a balanced mix of on-site meetings and home office.

We support you in your development

We offer you optimal conditions to familiarize yourself so that you can quickly find your way around and become a part of the team! To further your personal development, we offer you access to a modern learning environment - independent of time and location. This way you can also face the challenges of tomorrow!

We are committed to a net-zero future

After reaching our Green Pledge climate target in 2023 by reducing carbon emissions in our own business operations by 50.4% compared to the base year 2019, we set ourselves ambitious net-zero climate targets. The targets have been validated by the Science Based Targets initiative. By 2050, DKV Mobility commits to reach net-zero greenhouse gas emissions across the value chain from a 2022 base year. You want to take climate action? Follow us on our road to net-zero.

We are a certified employer

Our employees trust us. As an employer, we regularly receive awards from independent institutes, e.g. as Top Employer Germany and as Great Place to Work!

Have we made you curious? Then we are looking forward to your application! If you still have open questions then please contact Kai Westerhof via Kai.Westerhof@dkv-mobility.com.
